title: Fix inlined `function automatic` local variables not initializing on each call

features.name: automatic function with queue local variable
features.description: Code should include a function marked automatic containing a queue local variable that is initialized and used on each call
features.snippet: function automatic int t_queue(); int q[$]; q.push_back(0); return q.size(); endfunction

features.name: automatic function with scalar local variable
features.description: Code should include a function marked automatic containing an int local variable that increments on each call
features.snippet: function automatic int t_scalar(); int x; ++x; return x; endfunction

features.name: automatic function with struct local variable
features.description: Code should include a function marked automatic containing a struct local variable that is modified on each call
features.snippet: typedef struct { int y; } Y; function automatic int t_struct(); Y y; ++y.y; return y.y; endfunction

features.name: automatic function with class local variable
features.description: Code should include a function marked automatic containing a class instance local variable that is created and modified on each call
features.snippet: class Z; int z; endclass function automatic int t_class(); Z z = new(); ++z.z; return z.z; endfunction

features.name: scoped block with queue variable in loop
features.description: Code should include a for-loop with a scoped block containing a queue variable that should be fresh on each iteration
features.snippet: for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i) begin int q[$]; q.push_back(i); $display("%p", q); end
